danger_signals.visual_form: Unique enemy sprite at level conclusion, guarding treasure
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring skill to overcome; progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Level-specific obstacles (water mechanics in Bermuda Triangle, Egyptian traps, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-specific danger tied to world location; platformer peril

danger_signals.visual_form: Shapeshifter transformation from humanoid villain to mechanical form
danger_signals.meaning: Deception visual cue; final boss danger masked as familiar enemy

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for recovery and resource acquisition

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered treasures and collectibles throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Reward visibility; progression measurement; player agency signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Cane enhancement items available via NPC
safety_signals.meaning: Escalating capability; safe progression path unlocking new areas

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Protagonist-controlled levels; locations tied to family history (Scotland as ancestral stage)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: D-1000 robot disguise; shapeshifter mechanical form reveals deception
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bermuda Triangle pirate ship; final stage antagonist territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Huey delivers torn map piece to Scrooge; family motivation framing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scrooge initiates travel across five distinct global locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each location features unique visual theme (Niagara Falls, Egypt, Mu, Scotland, Bermuda Triangle pirate ship)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level-specific mechanics and boss guardians; treasure collection requirement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Glomgold presence transitions from distant threat to direct confrontation; robot transformation signals tech-enabled villainy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Webby kidnapping triggers ransom scenario; false redemption before D-1000 reveal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All six map pieces assembled unlock hidden sixth stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional content access; alternative victory condition (treasure found vs. recovered)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Good ending: treasures recovered by Launchpad, Scrooge reveals hidden treasure under hat. Bad ending: Glomgold obtains treasure, named greatest adventurer
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monetary depletion determines narrative outcome; player resource management affects ending state

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct stages (Niagara Falls, pirate ship, Mu, Egypt, Scotland) communicate world geography and thematic variety through sprite work and background design
Fergus McDuck's torn treasure map serves as visual MacGuffin; map piece collection in treasure chests ties exploration to legacy discovery
Cane upgrades from Gyro Gearloose visually enable level revisits and hidden area access, communicating progression through capability expansion
Glomgold's pirate ship in Bermuda Triangle establishes villainous territory; final confrontation locale visually reinforces climactic stakes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Cane-based interaction with objects (lever pulling, cannon firing, springy flower crossing); hook hanging and raft traversal indicate adaptive response to environmental challenges
Money collection priority drives level revisits; store purchase decisions; hoarding behavior drives monetary depletion ending variant
Unaware antagonist pursuing treasure independently; unknown to player throughout mid-game
Kidnaps Webby; demands ransom (treasures); shapeshifts into mechanical form; attempts ship-sinking escape
Kidnapped by Glomgold post-five-stage completion; ransomed; rescued by Scrooge
Recovers sunken treasures post-ship destruction; provides emotional resolution

notes: DuckTales 2 communicates world-state primarily through non-linear stage selection and location-specific visual design. Danger manifests through environmental hazards and boss encounters tied to geography. Political/factional conflict centers on McDuck vs. Glomgold treasure rivalry, with the D-1000 reveal serving as a twist that recontextualizes antagonist threat level. The game uses resource management (money) as a mechanical manifestation of player agency affecting ending states. Narrative progression and optional content (sixth stage) are gated by artifact collection (map pieces), creating player-directed world-state discovery. The design philosophy mirrors Mega Man's stage-select structure applied to adventure narrative.
